title: 225. Implement Stack using Queues
question:
Implement the following operations of a stack using queues.

  • push(x) -- Push element x onto stack.
  • pop() -- Removes the element on top of the stack.
  • top() -- Get the top element.
  • empty() -- Return whether the stack is empty.

Example:

MyStack stack = new MyStack();

stack.push(1);
stack.push(2);  
stack.top();   // returns 2
stack.pop();   // returns 2
stack.empty(); // returns false

Notes:

  • You must use only standard operations of a queue -- which means only push to back, peek/pop from front, size, and is empty operations are valid.
  • Depending on your language, queue may not be supported natively. You may simulate a queue by using a list or deque (double-ended queue), as long as you use only standard operations of a queue.
  • You may assume that all operations are valid (for example, no pop or top operations will be called on an empty stack).
code: \u000Aclass MyStack(object):\u000A\u000A    def __init__(self):\u000A        import Queue\u000A        self.pri, self.sec \u003D Queue.PriorityQueue(), Queue.PriorityQueue()\u000A\u000A    def push(self, x):\u000A        self.pri.put(x)\u000A\u000A    def pop(self):\u000A        while True:\u000A            tmp \u003D self.pri.get() if not self.pri.empty() else None\u000A            if not self.pri.empty(): self.sec.put(tmp)\u000A            else:\u000A                self.pri, self.sec \u003D self.sec, self.pri\u000A                return tmp\u000A            \u000A\u000A    def top(self):\u000A        while True:\u000A            tmp \u003D self.pri.get() if not self.pri.empty() else None\u000A            if tmp is not None: self.sec.put(tmp)\u000A            if self.pri.empty():\u000A                self.pri, self.sec \u003D self.sec, self.pri\u000A                return tmp\u000A\u000A    def empty(self):\u000A        return self.pri.empty()\u000A\u000A\u000A# Your MyStack object will be instantiated and called as such:\u000A# obj \u003D MyStack()https://leetcode.com/submissions/detail/150386250/\u000A# obj.push(x)\u000A# param_2 \u003D obj.pop()\u000A# param_3 \u003D obj.top()\u000A# param_4 \u003D obj.empty()
